summary: Re-tested text tool bug fix and full regression of Whiteboard. Text tool now works perfectly via real mouse.click: textarea mounts, retains focus (no spurious blur close), accepts typed input ('Hello World', 'Second line'), commits on Enter, scene-count increments correctly to '1 obj' then '2 obj', tool auto-switches to select, and DELETE button is visible. All other flows verified working: rectangle drag, pen drag, line, arrow, undo/redo, theme toggle, save to localStorage, export JSON, export PNG (with elements present), delete-selected via DELETE button. 15/15 critical flows PASS. Bug fix confirmed.
critical_code_review_comments: Text bug fix is correct and minimal: (1) handling text tool before setPointerCapture, (2) 250ms blur-guard with rAF re-focus, (3) double-rAF focus effect. No side effects observed.
Minor: the 250ms blur-guard is a heuristic; could fail on very slow devices. Acceptable for now.
Whiteboard.jsx still 1471 lines — splitting into modules remains recommended (carry-over from iteration_1).
Continuous-erase still pushes per-element history during a drag — carry-over recommendation.
seed_data_creation: localStorage cleared before test run (e1-canvas-studio-scene-v1, e1-canvas-studio-theme).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Text tool now works reliably with real page.mouse.click() in Playwright (no need for synthetic PointerEvent dispatch as workaround). Storage key: 'e1-canvas-studio-scene-v1'. Auto-save 400ms after each change.
rca of the issue: Root cause was a spurious blur fired immediately after the textarea mounted (browser shifted focus during the same click sequence that opened the editor), which triggered commitText() with an empty value and closed the textarea before typing could occur. Fix: (a) reorder handlePointerDown so the text branch runs before setPointerCapture (avoids capture interfering with focus), (b) onBlur ignores blur events that fire within 250ms of openedAt and re-focuses via rAF, (c) the focus useEffect uses double-rAF to outlast the click-sequence focus shift. Verified by typing 'Hello World' and 'Second line' end-to-end with Enter commits.
